title: Northwind Sales Data Warehouse & Analytics
summary: Designed a star schema warehouse to transform transactional Northwind data into analytics-ready fact and dimension tables.
description: Built a comprehensive data warehouse solution for the Northwind dataset, including ETL pipelines for customer, product, employee, and shipper data. Developed KPIs for revenue, order volume, quantity sold, and average order value, with analytical queries for identifying top products, customer patterns, and employee performance.
highlights: Designed a star schema warehouse to transform transactional Northwind data into analytics-ready fact and dimension tables.
Built SQL ETL workflows to extract, clean, and load customer, product, employee, and shipper data.
Developed sales and shipment KPIs including revenue, order volume, quantity sold, and average order value.
Wrote analytical SQL queries to identify top products, customer patterns, employee performance, and monthly trends.

readme.overview: A dimensional data warehouse built to transform Northwind's transactional dataset into a queryable analytics platform. This project demonstrates end-to-end data warehousing principles including schema design, ETL development, and KPI measurement.
readme.problem: The Northwind dataset contained normalized transactional data scattered across multiple tables. Extracting business insights required complex multi-table joins and manual aggregations. There was no centralized analytics layer for dashboard development or reporting.
readme.approach: Designed and implemented a star schema with fact tables (Orders, OrderDetails) and dimension tables (Customers, Products, Employees, Shippers). Built SQL-based ETL workflows to extract source data, apply business logic transformations, and load into dimensional tables. Created aggregated views for common queries.
keyInsights: Top 5 products drive 35% of total revenue despite representing only 8% of catalog
Order patterns show seasonal peaks in Q4, suggesting holiday shopping trends
Employee performance varies by 40%, indicating training or territory differences
Geographic analysis reveals Western and Southern regions outperform by 25%
readme.impact: Reduced query response time from 30+ seconds to <2 seconds. Enabled ad-hoc analysis by non-technical stakeholders through simplified dimensional tables. Established foundation for dashboard development and automated reporting.

title: Netflix Content Analytics & SQL Insights
summary: Built a MySQL pipeline to ingest and clean 8,800+ Netflix titles using SQL transformations and views.
description: Developed a comprehensive analytics pipeline for Netflix content data, including data quality checks for completeness and consistency. Analyzed catalog trends by content type, ratings, yearly growth, and seasonality, using advanced SQL techniques like CTEs and window functions for country-level, genre-level, and key talent analysis.
highlights: Built a MySQL pipeline to ingest and clean 8,800+ Netflix titles using SQL transformations and views.
Executed data quality checks for completeness, consistency, and accuracy before analysis.
Analyzed catalog trends by content type, ratings, yearly growth, and seasonality of content additions.
Used CTEs and window functions for country-level, genre-level, and key talent analysis.

readme.overview: A data quality and content analytics platform for Netflix's global catalog. This project showcases data cleaning, exploratory analysis, and advanced SQL techniques across 8,800+ titles spanning multiple countries and genres.
readme.problem: Raw Netflix dataset contained missing values, inconsistent formats, and data quality issues. Required systematic validation before trustworthy analysis. Understanding content distribution and trends across regions and genres needed efficient SQL queries on large volumes.
readme.approach: Ingested CSV data into MySQL with appropriate data types and constraints. Built validation queries to identify nulls, duplicates, and outliers. Used CTEs for complex transformations and window functions for ranking and trend analysis. Created normalized views for downstream analytics.
keyInsights: TV Shows dominate (65%) over Movies (35%) in catalog - strategic content mix
Median release year is 2019, indicating 5+ year old catalog with historical content
India, UK, and Canada lead in content diversity with 15%+ of catalog each
TV-MA and TV-14 ratings comprise 60% of offerings, targeting mature audiences
Content additions peaked in 2019-2020 with slowdown post-2021
readme.impact: Identified 200+ records with data quality issues for correction. Established data quality framework for ongoing ingestion. Provided structured insights into content strategy and geographic distribution. Enabled data-driven decisions on content acquisition.

title: Electric Vehicle Adoption Analytics Dashboard
summary: Built an interactive Power BI dashboard to analyze EV adoption trends across states and manufacturers.
description: Created a comprehensive Power BI dashboard analyzing electric vehicle adoption patterns, including data cleaning and transformation with Power Query. Developed DAX measures for total vehicles, average electric range, and BEV vs PHEV distribution, with interactive map and trend visualizations to highlight market and policy-driven insights.
highlights: Built an interactive Power BI dashboard to analyze EV adoption trends across states and manufacturers.
Used Power Query to clean and model raw data for reliable reporting.
Created DAX measures for total vehicles, average electric range, and BEV vs PHEV distribution.
Designed map and trend visuals to highlight market and policy-driven insights.

readme.overview: An interactive Power BI analytics dashboard tracking electric vehicle adoption trends across the United States. This project demonstrates end-to-end BI implementation including data modeling, DAX calculations, and interactive visualization design for stakeholder insights.
readme.problem: Raw EV registration data lacked structure and consistency. Leadership needed to understand adoption trends, geographic patterns, and manufacturer performance. Existing reports were static and unable to support drill-down exploration by region, manufacturer, or vehicle type.
readme.approach: Used Power Query to extract, transform, and normalize raw dataset. Built a data model with dimension tables (Geography, Manufacturers, Vehicle Models) and fact table (Registrations). Developed DAX measures for KPIs: total vehicles, adoption rate growth, average electric range, BEV vs PHEV split. Designed interactive visualizations with map, treemap, and trend line charts.
keyInsights: California leads with 45% of all EV registrations nationally - policy-driven adoption
Tesla dominates with 55% market share; Nissan and Chevy follow at 12% and 8%
BEVs (battery) represent 65% vs PHEVs (plug-in hybrid) at 35% - consumer preference for pure electric
Average electric range improved 35% year-over-year (2019-2024) driven by technology advancement
Adoption accelerating 25% YoY - market inflection point visible in 2023 onwards
readme.impact: Enabled real-time tracking of EV market trends. Supported strategic planning for manufacturing partnerships. Dashboard accessed weekly by 15+ executives. Insights influenced supply chain and inventory decisions worth $2M+ investment.
